steps.text: Evaluate the licensee’s use of controls for radiation areas unique to Research and Test reactors, such as pneumatic irradiation sample transfer systems, beam ports, and other potential radiation work areas.
Evaluate the licensee’s use of shielding and controls used to remove or modify shielding.
Observe the protective clothing worn by two facility experimenters or other respective personnel during their work activities.
Conduct discussions with two individuals to determine their use of protective clothing.
Verify that personnel are instructed in the proper use and function of the protective clothing in accordance with 10 CFR 19.12.
Verify that the licensee has provisions for the correct disposition of contaminated protective clothing.

Observe respective activities and discuss the radiation safety training received by two permanent facility workers (and where applicable, two experimenters or students) to confirm appropriate training was received and understood.

Accompany a person qualified in the licensee's radiation control techniques on normal rounds, or request to be shown the manner in which such tasks are normally accomplished, to observe two radiological surveys, sampling, or monitoring activities.
Determine by direct observation and discussion whether personnel conducting these activities are qualified by training and experience.
Examine the licensee's training records to confirm that technicians are sufficiently trained.
Verify whether surveys were performed as required by the licensee's procedures and if exposure rates are consistent with the ALARA program.
Determine whether radiation levels are above those specified in 10 CFR Part 20, Subpart D.
Verify if the licensee evaluated personnel occupancy of these areas and the potential dose to occupants.
Perform independent radiological surveys as needed to verify licensee assumptions or measurements.
Compare inspector survey measurements with those completed by the licensee in the same areas to verify consistency.
Review surveys conducted by the licensee during both shutdown and operations following changes in shielding, and verify agreement with records.
Verify how the licensee assures that airborne contamination levels are within the regulatory requirements of 10 CFR Part 20, Subparts C and D.
Examine surveys of solid waste material to determine whether the licensee complies with their procedures and requirements.
Verify that all survey, sampling, and monitoring instruments have current calibrations appropriate for the types and energies of radiation to be detected.
Examine the technical adequacy of calibration procedures at facilities that perform their own calibrations.
Verify ALARA implementation for one routinely performed experiment or process having an actual or potential for a relatively high radiological exposure.
Discuss ALARA considerations with operations or radiation protection personnel to determine the effectiveness of the licensee's ALARA program.
Review and audit the radiation protection program over a two-three year cycle (or annually) to cover program content and implementation.
Verify if facility supervisors periodically perform observations of staff effectiveness in radiological work practices, work monitoring, procedural compliance, and survey adequacy.
Verify if the radiation protection program is periodically reviewed and audits are performed by the radiation safety group.
Verify if the safety committee audits and evaluates the radiation protection program to assure compliance.

preconditions.text: Observation of protective clothing worn by personnel is possible.
Observation of protective clothing worn by personnel is not practical.
If concerns arise regarding technician qualifications
Before using any instrumentation, ensure operability checks (e.g., source and battery checks) are performed.
If radiation levels above background are suspected in areas outside of the restricted area
If radiation levels above those specified in 10 CFR Part 20, Subpart D are detected in uncontrolled areas
If independent surveys are performed by an inspector
If the potential exists for airborne contamination
If the facility performs its own calibrations
The ALARA inspection need not be repeated if it was covered under other inspection requirements and received adequate evaluation.

constraints.text: Do not enter a high radiation area without a licensee escort trained in radiation protection who is knowledgeable of the high radiation area and the health physics precautions to be taken.

Protective clothing (e.g., gloves, booties, rubber boots, hair covers, lab coats, coveralls, and rubber or plastic aprons) must be used in accordance with licensee procedures or precautions posted at the entrance to controlled areas.
All instrumentation used during these activities are required to be operable, calibrated, and maintained as required by 10 CFR 20.1501(b) and the licensee's procedures.
Particular attention is to be given to beam ports, experimental stations, and ion exchangers for cleanup of primary water, where applicable.
Good housekeeping practices suggest a barrier any time that contamination levels exceed double the background unless the contamination is removable and the area is immediately decontaminated.
Records for survey, sampling and monitoring are required to be maintained in accordance with 10 CFR 20.2103.
Information Notice 85-92, 'Surveys of Wastes Before Disposal from Nuclear Reactor Facilities,' is to be used as technical guidance.
ANSI N323, 'Radiation Protection Instrumentation Test and Calibration,' 1978, provides additional guidance for calibration procedures.
Use respiratory protection equipment (and other controls) to limit intakes of radioactive material consistent with ALARA.
